title: Returning a Book to the Library
summary: A student returns a library book late and the librarian explains the late fee policy.

body.speaker: Student
body.text: Hello. I would like to return this book, please.

body.speaker: Librarian
body.text: Of course! Let me check the due date for you.

body.speaker: Student
body.text: I think it might be a little late. I am sorry about that.

body.speaker: Librarian
body.text: Let me see. Yes, it was due last Tuesday. Today is Friday.

body.speaker: Student
body.text: Oh no! How many days late is it?

body.speaker: Librarian
body.text: It is three days late. We have a small late fee of twenty cents per day.

body.speaker: Student
body.text: So that is sixty cents in total?

body.speaker: Librarian
body.text: Yes, that is correct. Sixty cents, please.

body.speaker: Student
body.text: Here you are. I am really sorry. I got very busy with school.

body.speaker: Librarian
body.text: That is okay! It happens. Did you enjoy the book?

body.speaker: Student
body.text: Yes, I loved it! It was about a boy who travels to a magic island.

body.speaker: Librarian
body.text: That sounds wonderful! Would you like to borrow another book today?

body.speaker: Student
body.text: Yes, please! Do you have any books about space?

body.speaker: Librarian
body.text: We have several. Follow me to the science section and I will show you.

body.speaker: Student
body.text: Thank you so much! You are very helpful.

annotations.exampleEn: Please return the book before the due date.
The due date for this book is next Monday.
You will have to pay a late fee if you do not return it on time.
The charge is one dollar per day.
How much is it in total?
I forgot to call because I got very busy at work.
The story is about a magic island far away.
Can I borrow a pen from you?
He loves books about space and the planets.
The science section is on the third shelf.
There are several good books about animals in the library.
The librarian was very helpful and found the book quickly.

grammarNotes.title: Simple arithmetic in English
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Trong tiếng Anh, phép nhân đơn giản có thể diễn đạt như sau: 'twenty cents per day, three days = sixty cents.' Dùng 'in total' để chỉ tổng số: 'That is sixty cents in total.'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Simple calculations in English can be expressed as: 'twenty cents per day, three days = sixty cents.' Use 'in total' to state the sum: 'That is sixty cents in total.'

grammarNotes.title: Would like to for polite requests
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Would like to + verb' dùng cho yêu cầu lịch sự: 'I would like to return this book.' / 'Would you like to borrow another book?' Đây là cách nói lịch sự phổ biến nhất trong giao tiếp hàng ngày.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Would like to + verb' is used for polite requests: 'I would like to return this book.' / 'Would you like to borrow another book?' It is the most common polite form in everyday conversation.

translationVi: Học sinh: Xin chào. Tôi muốn trả cuốn sách này, làm ơn. Thủ thư: Tất nhiên! Để tôi kiểm tra ngày hạn cho bạn. Học sinh: Tôi nghĩ nó có thể hơi trễ một chút. Tôi xin lỗi về điều đó. Thủ thư: Để tôi xem. Vâng, hạn trả là thứ Ba tuần trước. Hôm nay là thứ Sáu. Học sinh: Ôi không! Trễ bao nhiêu ngày vậy? Thủ thư: Trễ ba ngày. Chúng tôi có một khoản phí trễ hạn nhỏ là hai mươi xu mỗi ngày. Học sinh: Vậy tổng cộng là sáu mươi xu? Thủ thư: Đúng vậy. Sáu mươi xu, làm ơn. Học sinh: Của bạn đây. Tôi thực sự rất xin lỗi. Tôi bận học rất nhiều. Thủ thư: Không sao! Chuyện đó xảy ra thôi. Bạn có thích cuốn sách không? Học sinh: Vâng, tôi rất thích! Nó kể về một cậu bé du lịch đến một hòn đảo ma thuật. Thủ thư: Nghe hay quá! Hôm nay bạn có muốn mượn cuốn sách khác không? Học sinh: Vâng, làm ơn! Bạn có sách về vũ trụ không? Thủ thư: Chúng tôi có một vài cuốn. Theo tôi đến khu vực khoa học và tôi sẽ chỉ cho bạn. Học sinh: Cảm ơn bạn rất nhiều! Bạn thật hữu ích.

questions.prompt: Why does the student go to the library?
questions.explanation: 'I would like to return this book, please.'
questions.hint: Look at the student's first line.

questions.prompt: When was the book due to be returned?
questions.hint: Find the librarian's statement about the due date.

questions.prompt: What day is it today in the story?
questions.hint: Look for the sentence about today's date.

questions.prompt: How many days late is the book?
How much is the late fee per day?
questions.explanation: 'We have a small late fee of twenty cents per day.'
questions.hint: Find the sentence about the fee.

questions.prompt: What is the total late fee the student must pay?
questions.explanation: 'So that is sixty cents in total?' 'Yes, that is correct.'
questions.hint: Multiply the daily fee by the number of days late.

questions.prompt: Why was the book returned late?
options: The student was busy with school

options: A girl who visits a forest
A boy who travels to a magic island
A dog who goes on adventures
A family on a space mission
questions.explanation: 'It was about a boy who travels to a magic island.'

questions.prompt: What topic does the student want to borrow a book about?
questions.explanation: 'Do you have any books about space?'
questions.hint: Find the student's new book request.

questions.prompt: Where does the librarian take the student?
questions.explanation: 'Follow me to the science section and I will show you.'
questions.hint: Find the librarian's instruction at the end.

questions.prompt: Did the student enjoy the returned book?
questions.hint: Find the student's opinion of the book.

questions.prompt: How does the student describe the librarian at the end?
questions.hint: Look at the student's last line.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The student had to pay a late fee for returning the book late. Why do you think libraries have late fees? Do you think late fees are a good idea?
